title: Intriguing Buttery Turkish Matcha Pide
description: A buttery and springy Turkish dish featuring matcha, perfect for a festive vegetarian meal, made using a pressure cooker to create a crispy crust and a soft interior. The matcha adds a unique and intriguing flavor to this traditional Turkish pide.
instructions: In a large mixing bowl, combine the flour, matcha powder, salt, sugar, and yeast.
Gradually add the warm water to the dry ingredients and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with a damp cloth, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Preheat the pressure cooker to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Punch down the dough and shape it into a pide, brushing the top with the melted butter and beaten egg.
Place the pide in the pressure cooker and cook for 20 minutes.
Let the pide rest for 5 minutes before slicing and serving.
